channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Considers Peace Talks as Ceasefire Deadline Nears
item.description: Iran is evaluating participation in US peace talks in Pakistan following moves to end a US blockade of Iranian ports. The two-week ceasefire is set to expire, with uncertainty growing over the fate of negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Promises Better Nuclear Deal Than 2015 Agreement
item.description: President Trump said the new nuclear deal being negotiated with Iran will be superior to the 2015 international agreement, as his administration pushes for a resolution before the ceasefire expires.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Fitch Cuts Philippines Outlook to Negative on Energy Shock
item.description: Credit ratings agency Fitch revised the Philippines outlook to negative from stable, citing medium-term growth risks from disrupted public investment and high exposure to the global energy crisis.

item.title: ⛽ EU Has Five Months of Jet Fuel Reserves, Dutch Government Says
item.description: The Dutch government estimated the European Union has enough kerosene supply to last about five months, drawing on domestic production and strategic reserves amid global energy disruptions.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Indian Rupee Falls as Markets Brace for Ceasefire Expiry
item.description: The Indian rupee logged its steepest one-day fall in a week as wary investors braced for the end of the US-Iran ceasefire amid rising Middle East tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran signals no plans to join US peace talks as ceasefire deadline looms
item.description: Iran says it has no plans to send negotiators to Islamabad for a new round of talks with the United States, citing US violations of the ceasefire including the naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. Less than 48 hours remain before the fragile ceasefire expires.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU hosts Gaza peace conference as Orbans defeat opens path for action on Israel
item.description: More than 60 nations sent representatives to Brussels for talks on stability and peace in Gaza and the West Bank. Hungarys election defeat of Israel ally Viktor Orban removes a key veto that had blocked EU sanctions on Israeli settlers and other actions.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Pro-Russian ex-president Radev wins Bulgaria election in landslide
item.description: Rumen Radevs Kremlin-friendly party won 44.6% of the vote in Bulgarias snap parliamentary elections, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member closer to Moscows orbit. The victory represents a major shift in Bulgarian politics.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel and Lebanon engage in direct negotiations amid Iran ceasefire
item.description: A separate but intertwined conflict shows signs of progress as Israel and Lebanon engage in direct negotiations with a temporary ceasefire in place. The talks involve the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Bulgaria Enters Uncharted Territory as Pro-Russian Radev Wins Landslide Election
item.description: Former President Rumen Radev's Progressive Bulgaria won 44.7% in Bulgaria's eighth parliamentary election in five years, securing the first absolute majority since 1997. The result raises questions about Bulgaria's EU and NATO alignment as Radev pursues dialogue with Russia.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish FM Fidan Slams Greek Cypriots and Israel for Alliance Against Muslim Countries
item.description: At the Antalya Diplomacy Forum, Hakan Fidan criticized the alliance between Greek Cypriots and Israel, saying it undermines regional stability. He said Turkey seeks to "extinguish conflicts" while Israel forms partnerships against Muslim nations.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky Criticizes US Envoys for Visiting Moscow Without Kyiv Stop
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelensky called it "disrespectful" that US envoys Witkoff and Kushner have flown to Moscow multiple times but have not yet visited Kyiv. He said the quickest way to end the war would be a ceasefire along current lines, but Russia is unwilling.

item.title: 🇫🇷 French Conservatives Select Bruno Retailleau as 2027 Presidential Candidate
item.description: Les Républicains party members selected party president Bruno Retailleau as their candidate for the 2027 French presidential election with 73.8% support. The 65-year-old described his candidacy as born from "duty" rather than ambition.

item.title: 🇹🇷🇳🇬 Turkey to Train 200 Nigerian Special Forces Under New Defense Agreement
item.description: Nigeria and Turkey signed a defense pact to help fight Nigeria's 17-year jihadist insurgency. Turkey will train 200 Nigerian special forces soldiers as part of the agreement.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Rejects Any Transfer of Enriched Uranium Stockpiles in Nuclear Talks
item.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson stated Monday that at no stage of current or previous negotiations has Iran agreed to transfer or dilute its enriched uranium stockpiles, signaling continued tension ahead of ceasefire deadline.

item.title: 🇧🇬 Pro-Russian Former President Radev Wins Bulgarian Election in Landslide
item.description: Rumen Radev, Bulgaria's Kremlin-friendly former president, has won Sunday's parliamentary election by a landslide, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member state closer to Moscow's orbit.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Deploys Warships for Pacific Drills as Regional Tensions Rise
item.description: A Chinese naval task group will conduct combat drills in the Western Pacific, coinciding with joint US-Philippines-Japan military exercises that Beijing says risk eroding regional trust.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Entrenches Control Over South Lebanon, Warns Residents to Stay Out
item.description: Israel told residents of south Lebanon to stay out of a border territory belt and not approach the Litani River area, cementing its grip despite a ceasefire with Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Warns US-Philippines-Japan Military Drills Risk Undermining Regional Trust
item.description: China cautioned against military cooperation that could erode trust as the US and Philippines began annual exercises with expanded Japanese participation.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines and US Launch Military Exercises Near Taiwan Testing Real-World Readiness
item.description: Philippine and US forces began annual exercises including maritime strike drills on a remote island near Taiwan, testing readiness under real-world conditions.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskiy Says Druzhba Oil Pipeline to Be Restored by End of April
item.description: Ukraine president said the damaged Druzhba pipeline carrying Russian oil to Eastern Europe will be operational again by the end of April.

item.title: 🛢️ Kremlin Says Russian Oil Exports Cannot Be Ignored After US Sanctions Waiver
item.description: The Kremlin commented on a US sanctions waiver for some Russian oil exports, saying Russia is a responsible global energy player whose volumes cannot be dismissed.

item.title: ☢️🇷🇺🇮🇷 Russia evacuates over 600 employees from Irans Bushehr nuclear plant
item.description: Rosatom chief Likhachev confirms only 20 volunteers remain at Bushehr with 4 in Tehran as Russia completes evacuation of nuclear personnel from Iran amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷🇵🇰 Trump says Iran deal to be signed tonight in Islamabad
item.description: President Trump announced an Iran deal would be signed tonight in Islamabad. No Iranian response has been given to Trumps claim yet.

item.title: 🇳🇴🇪🇺⛽ EU lawmakers urge Norway to share windfall energy profits amid Mideast tensions
item.description: Calls grow for Norway to share increased energy profits as the Iran conflict and fragile ceasefire push prices higher, boosting Norways export earnings.
